family_name: Prime labelings of zero-divisor graph families
strongest_honest_claim: The zero-divisor campaign now has paired verified p = 11 feeders on both active lines, a verified p = 13 feeder on the four-class Γ(Z_p × Z_25) line, checked Lean names for the paired abstract support-template reductions, and a checked reusable singleton-one lemma on the Γ(Z_p × Z_p × Z_2) line. The strongest honest slice is now that prime labelability of Γ(Z_p × Z_25) and Γ(Z_p × Z_p × Z_2) reduces to explicit classwise coprimality partitions on tiny support graphs, that the second family collapses to three parameter-sensitive interfaces after setting C = 1, and that the first p = 13 four-class stress test survives once the C block is refined to allow two doubled-prime exceptions into D. What remains is to connect those abstractions to checked support decompositions of the actual zero-divisor families and decide whether the six-class arithmetic program also survives at p = 13.
paper_title_hint: Support-template reductions and refined prime-block arithmetic in two zero-divisor prime-labeling families
theorem_slice_target: Paired support-template reduction theorems for Γ(Z_p × Z_25) and Γ(Z_p × Z_p × Z_2), with the second theorem stated on the actual support graph A-B, A-C, A-F, B-C, B-E, C-D and reduced to three parameter-sensitive interfaces after setting C = 1.
primary_theorem_slice_statement: For odd prime p, prime labelability of Γ(Z_p × Z_25) and Γ(Z_p × Z_p × Z_2) reduces to explicit classwise interval partitions on tiny support graphs; on the four-class family, the C block may include a bounded number of primes below half the interval provided their nontrivial multiples stay out of A ∪ B, and on the second family the actual support graph is A-B, A-C, A-F, B-C, B-E, C-D so that after setting the singleton class C to label 1 only A-B, A-F, and B-E remain parameter-sensitive.
fallback_theorem_slice_statement: If the paired theorem slice still does not close, preserve the first honest p = 13 breakpoint as a theorem about the arithmetic program: the old upper-half-prime-only F25 C subtemplate already fails even though Γ(Z_13 × Z_25) remains prime with a refined C block, and the naive {2,3}-smooth F2 template is still the next zero-slack test.
parameter_sensitive_steps: Supply p - 1 C-labels for Γ(Z_p × Z_25) while allowing a bounded number of sub-half primes whose nontrivial multiples are pushed into D and still keeping A ∪ B clear of every C-prime factor.
Choose a four-label sparse barrier set for the B clique in Γ(Z_p × Z_25) whose nontrivial multiples spill into at most the 20 A-slots.
Count enough smooth labels for A ∪ E in Γ(Z_p × Z_p × Z_2) while keeping B as a disjoint large-prime block; the naive {2,3}-smooth template is now the first zero-slack p = 13 case to audit.
Keep the F block away from the prime support assigned to A in Γ(Z_p × Z_p × Z_2).
Show the leftover D block remains unconstrained once the required interfaces are satisfied.
fallback_target: If the arithmetic corollaries still do not close, preserve the smallest honest theorem about the arithmetic program: the unchanged upper-half-prime-only F25 C block already fails at p = 13 even though the graph stays prime after refinement, and the naive F2 {2,3}-smooth program is the next zero-slack candidate for failure.
next_blocker: The Lean surface now contains the checked singleton-one reduction lemma and the four-class line has survived p = 13 with a refined C block, but the campaign still lacks checked support-decomposition lemmas from zero-divisor structure and still needs the decisive six-class p = 13 audit at Γ(Z_13 × Z_13 × Z_2).
next_feeder_why_decisive: With Γ(Z_13 × Z_25) now verified, the remaining first p = 13 frontier is the six-class Γ(Z_p × Z_p × Z_2) line. Γ(Z_13 × Z_13 × Z_2) is decisive because the naive {2,3}-smooth reservoir is exactly zero-slack there, so success or failure will sharply determine how much paired p-range language the campaign can honestly use.
